# lattice dependencies tiering

## Summary
The pre-existing `dependencies/` namespace had ~40 pages in the live `mono-repo-vault`, many skeletal. The §2.1 redesign tiers them: an **auto-generated index** covers every dep across the monorepo (one row per dep); **detail pages are opt-in** when a dep is load-bearing or quirky enough to earn narrative; a **`kind:` discriminator** distinguishes packages from services with slightly different frontmatter shapes.

Captured in 2026-05-lattice-ecosystem-schema-refinements §2.1 (`raw/specs/lattice-ecosystem-schema-refinements.md:343-478`).

## The three tiers

### 1. `dependencies/index.md` (auto-generated, every dep)

Regenerated by `scan_monorepo.py` on every scan. One markdown table covering every dep across the monorepo. Service rows merge in from a hand-maintained `<vault>/dependencies/services.yaml` (services aren't in `package.json`).

| Name | Kind | Ecosystem/Provider | Family | Versions | Used by | Detail |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| tailwindcss | package | npm | tailwind | 3.4.0 | web-next-ts | `[[wiki/tailwind]]` |
| autoprefixer | package | npm | *(tailwind co-req)* | 10.4.16 | web-next-ts | — |
| MongoDB Atlas | service | mongodb-atlas | — | n/a | location-aws-node-ts | `[[wiki/mongodb-atlas]]` |

Flat layout (not grouped sections) keeps the index grep-able and lets viewers do their own grouping. The index is regenerated, never hand-edited. Marker-bounded so manual notes can sit alongside.

### 2. Detail pages (opt-in)

Triggers for creating one:
- Documented quirks (workarounds, version-specific bugs, integration gotchas)
- Version skew across packages worth reconciling
- Subject of an active issue or ADR
- A package family worth narrating as a unit

Lint can *suggest* detail pages via the drift report; it never auto-creates them. Auto-creating stubs is the failure mode this whole change targets.

### 3. The `kind:` discriminator

Two values: `package | service`. Frontmatter shape diverges per kind.

**`kind: package`** — `ecosystem` field (`npm | pypi | cargo | go | brew | system`); `versions_in_use` list; `family` back-pointer if member of a family.

**`kind: service`** — `service_name`; `provider:` (`aws | gcp | azure | mongodb-atlas | cloudflare | github | …`); no `versions_in_use` (services aren't versioned the same way).

`load_bearing: true` is explicit on every detail page — the existence of a detail page *is* the load-bearing signal; making the field explicit lets lint catch detail pages whose `load_bearing: false` got set without the page being deleted.

## `dependency_layer` lint rules (10)

| Rule | Severity | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dep-kind-not-in-enum` | error | `kind:` outside the three-value set |
| `dep-package-without-ecosystem` | error | `kind: package` and `ecosystem:` missing |
| `dep-service-without-provider` | error | `kind: service` and `provider:` missing |
| `dep-family-member-not-in-scan` | error | a member listed in `members:` isn't found in any manifest |
| `dep-family-back-pointer-mismatch` | error | package has `family: X` but `X` family page doesn't list it (or vice versa) |
| `dep-multiple-families` | error | a package is claimed by two different family pages |
| `dep-detail-without-load-bearing` | warn | detail page exists but `load_bearing: true` not set |
| `dep-stub-detail-page` | warn | detail page body <15 lines beyond frontmatter |
| `dep-index-stale` | warn | `dependencies/index.md` regen marker older than newest scan-detected dep change |

All gated behind `--check dependency_layer`; promoted to default after the live vault is migrated.

## Migration

For an existing live vault's dep pages:
1. Scan run regenerates `dependencies/index.md`.
2. `migrate_dependencies.py` keeps any page with substantive body content (>15 lines beyond frontmatter); deletes stubs.
3. Detects `<family>-<member>` and `@<family>/<member>` patterns; surfaces family candidates in `migration-audit.md` for human acceptance (no auto-creation).
4. Wikilinks to deleted stub pages either rewrite to `[[wiki/dependencies/index]]` or surface as broken-link audit entries.
